article_meta.title: War in UkraineKremlin says leaked U.S

reasoning: The model correctly flagged bias in the author's own prose without attributing quotes to the author and applied mostly appropriate labels. It missed some additional biased phrasing in the author's narration (e.g., 'pressuring Kyiv,' 'maximalist demands') but overall avoided major errors.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items come from the author's narration; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was treated as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items are defensible: subjective evaluation ('favor Moscow heavily') and emotive phrasing ('added fuel to criticism'). The source-attribution omission call is somewhat debatable but reasonable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable author-level bias such as 'pressuring Kyiv' (loaded verb) and 'maximalist demands' (subjective qualifier), and possibly 'shoot that down' (colloquial, combative phrasing).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels fit the instances: subjective qualifiers and emotional phrasing are correctly identified; the attribution omission label is defensible.
